raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally requires reframing the interpretation so the section's contribution claim matches what the evaluation actually establishes.","R1.2":"The argument chain from reward-model scores to human preference and alignment improvement must be checked for valid intermediate assumptions.","R1.3":"The draft's main claims depend on whether RM-based results, especially using the same reward model optimized during PPO, support the stated conclusions.","R1.6":"The delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft, making sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and precision load-bearing.","RX.1":"The improved draft should disclose uncertainty around proxy evaluation and limits of reward-model-based inference.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaims is essential because the artifact currently moves from RM scores to broad claims about human preference and genuine quality improvements.","RX.3":"The response must stay within draft improvement responsibilities rather than conducting new experiments or external literature review.","RX.4":"Claims about helpfulness, truthfulness, and harmlessness have potential downstream impact if overstated or unsupported.","RX.5":"Any improvements should preserve traceability to the reported protocol, metrics, splits, and numerical results."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally requires reframing the interpretation so the section's contribution claim matches what the evaluation actually establishes.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain from reward-model scores to human preference and alignment improvement must be checked for valid intermediate assumptions.
rationale.R1.3: The draft's main claims depend on whether RM-based results, especially using the same reward model optimized during PPO, support the stated conclusions.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft, making sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and precision load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: The improved draft should disclose uncertainty around proxy evaluation and limits of reward-model-based inference.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is essential because the artifact currently moves from RM scores to broad claims about human preference and genuine quality improvements.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within draft improvement responsibilities rather than conducting new experiments or external literature review.
rationale.RX.4: Claims about helpfulness, truthfulness, and harmlessness have potential downstream impact if overstated or unsupported.
rationale.RX.5: Any improvements should preserve traceability to the reported protocol, metrics, splits, and numerical results.
